notes_md:
**tự dưng** = suddenly, out of nowhere, for no clear reason. It often has a feeling of “for no reason” or “randomly”.

It’s an adverb, usually placed before the verb or after the subject.  

It’s more casual than **đột nhiên** or **bỗng** (those sound a bit more written or dramatic).  
Use it when something changes unexpectedly, especially feelings, actions, or situations.
exampleSentencesTranslated: Last night he suddenly called me at midnight.
Suddenly it rained heavily so we had to stop.
She suddenly feels sad when she hears this song.

example.en: Today, it suddenly started to rain.
example.vi: Hôm nay tự dưng trời mưa.
example.es: Hoy, de repente comenzó a llover.

notes:
tự dưng = suddenly, out of nowhere, for no clear reason. It often has a feeling of “for no reason” or “randomly”.

It’s an adverb, usually placed before the verb or after the subject.  

It’s more casual than đột nhiên or bỗng (those sound a bit more written or dramatic).  
Use it when something changes unexpectedly, especially feelings, actions, or situations.
exampleSentences: Tối qua anh ấy tự dưng gọi cho em lúc nửa đêm.
Tự dưng trời mưa to nên chúng ta phải dừng lại.
Chị tự dưng cảm thấy buồn khi nghe bài hát này.
